title: yosys-smtbmc: Problems with "$past()" function in "cover" statements
body:
I'm trying to generate witnesses with yosys-smtbmc and I'm not having much luck with $past() functions in cover statements.

Yosys-smtbmc reports these cover statements as unreachable, even though I can prove that they are.

For example, I would like to make sure that a signal ("wb_itr_mon_fsm_busy") can be asserted for two consecutive clock cycles ([see line 408 in the example below](https://github.com/hotwolf/WbXbc/blob/master/bench/verilog/ftb_WbXbc_address_decoder.sv)): 

```
   402     //Cover state transitions in monitor
   403     //==================================
   404     always @(posedge clk_i)
   405         begin
   406  //        cover (wb_itr_mon_fsm_busy & $past(wb_itr_mon_fsm_idle));
   407  //        cover (wb_itr_mon_fsm_idle & $past(wb_itr_mon_fsm_idle));
   408            cover (wb_itr_mon_fsm_busy & $past(wb_itr_mon_fsm_busy));
   409  //        cover (wb_itr_mon_fsm_idle & $past(wb_itr_mon_fsm_busy));
   410
   411            //Enforce witness
   412            cover (wb_itr_mon_fsm_busy & ~rst & req & ack);
   413         end // always @ (posedge clk_i)
```

When I run yosys-smtbmc (through SymbiYosys), it reports that the cover statement in line 408 is unreachable.
However, the cover statement in line 412 is reachable and generates a witness, which satisfies the cover statement in line 408:

```
SBY 21:50:55 [./tools/SymbiYosis/run/cover.WbXbc_address_decoder.default] engine_0: starting process "cd ./tools/SymbiYosis/run/cover.WbXbc_address_decoder.default; yosys-smtbmc --presat --unroll -c --noprogress -t 10 --append 8 --dump-vcd engine_0/trace%.vcd --dump-vlogtb engine_0/trace%_tb.v --dump-smtc engine_0/trace%.smtc model/design_smt2.smt2"
SBY 21:50:55 [./tools/SymbiYosis/run/cover.WbXbc_address_decoder.default] engine_0: ##   0:00:00  Solver: yices
SBY 21:50:56 [./tools/SymbiYosis/run/cover.WbXbc_address_decoder.default] engine_0: ##   0:00:00  Checking cover reachability in step 0..

SBY 21:50:59 [./tools/SymbiYosis/run/cover.WbXbc_address_decoder.default] engine_0: ##   0:00:03  Writing trace to VCD file: engine_0/trace0.vcd
SBY 21:51:00 [./tools/SymbiYosis/run/cover.WbXbc_address_decoder.default] engine_0: ##   0:00:04  Writing trace to Verilog testbench: engine_0/trace0_tb.v

SBY 21:51:00 [./tools/SymbiYosis/run/cover.WbXbc_address_decoder.default] engine_0: ##   0:00:04  Checking cover reachability in step 9..
SBY 21:51:00 [./tools/SymbiYosis/run/cover.WbXbc_address_decoder.default] engine_0: ##   0:00:04  Unreached cover statement at ftb_WbXbc_address_decoder.sv:408.
SBY 21:51:00 [./tools/SymbiYosis/run/cover.WbXbc_address_decoder.default] engine_0: ##   0:00:04  Status: FAILED (!)

SBY 21:51:00 [./tools/SymbiYosis/run/cover.WbXbc_address_decoder.default] summary: Elapsed clock time [H:MM:SS (secs)]: 0:00:06 (6)
SBY 21:51:00 [./tools/SymbiYosis/run/cover.WbXbc_address_decoder.default] summary: Elapsed process time [H:MM:SS (secs)]: 0:00:07 (7)
SBY 21:51:00 [./tools/SymbiYosis/run/cover.WbXbc_address_decoder.default] summary: engine_0 (smtbmc) returned FAIL
SBY 21:51:00 [./tools/SymbiYosis/run/cover.WbXbc_address_decoder.default] DONE (FAIL, rc=2)
```
I see the same problem when I try to to implement the $past() function manually with registern.

If you'd like to replay my example, please do the following:

```
git clone https://github.com/hotwolf/WbXbc.git
cd WbXbc
make cover.WbXbc_address_decoder
make debug
```
(Note: "make debug" requires the installation of Perl and the Verilog-Perl library.  Alternatively, run "make debug.list" to see the location of the VCD file and run gtkwave manually)
